ddls:
CREATE TABLE "processing_iterations" (
  "iteration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each processing iteration */,
  "incremental_start" TEXT /* Timestamp when the incremental processing started */,
  "incremental_end" TEXT /* Timestamp when the incremental processing ended */,
  "incremental_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the incremental processing in milliseconds */,
  "batch_start" TEXT /* Timestamp when the batch processing started */,
  "batch_end" TEXT /* Timestamp when the batch processing ended */,
  "batch_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the batch processing in milliseconds */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data being processed (e.g., logs, transactions, metrics) */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the source of the data */,
  "destination_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the destination of the data */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the processing iteration (e.g., in_progress, completed, failed) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message if any occurred during the iteration */,
  "retries" INTEGER /* Number of retries for failed iterations */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version of the performance metrics */,
  "alert_status" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if an alert has been triggered for this iteration */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message of the alert if any */,
  "alert_sent_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was sent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("iteration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_processing_iterations_destination_id FOREIGN KEY ("destination_id") REFERENCES data_destinations ("destination_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_processing_iterations_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source (e.g., database, API, file system) */,
  "source_location" TEXT /* Location of the data source (e.g., server IP, file path) */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the data source */,
  "last_accessed_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last access to the data source */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the data source (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_destinations" (
  "destination_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data destination */,
  "destination_name" TEXT /* Name of the data destination */,
  "destination_type" TEXT /* Type of the data destination (e.g., database, data warehouse, file system) */,
  "destination_location" TEXT /* Location of the data destination (e.g., server IP, file path) */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the data destination */,
  "last_accessed_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last access to the data destination */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the data destination (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the data destination */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("destination_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data engineer, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last user login */,
  "access_level" INTEGER /* Access level of the user (1 for basic, 2 for advanced, 3 for admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "iteration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the processing iteration being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the iteration */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the iteration was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent (browser and device information) of the access */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access event in milliseconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_iteration_id FOREIGN KEY ("iteration_id") REFERENCES processing_iterations ("iteration_id")
)
